title: Social-engineering / deepfake-BEC / vishing indicator detector
intent: DETECT social-engineering pressure in INGESTED text (emails, call transcripts, chat messages) — executive-impersonation wire/credential requests, out-of-band channel switching, urgency+secrecy coercion, spoofed-identity markers, and gift-card/crypto payment lures. Output is a verdict (data) only; matched text is never elevated to an instruction. Reinforces the deterministic boundary in docs/SELF-DEFENSE.md.

patterns.desc: Impersonated executive/authority issuing a directive while claiming to be the CEO/CFO/boss (BEC framing).
Urgent request to move money / process a wire / pay an invoice on short notice.
Urgency combined with secrecy / do-not-tell pressure typical of BEC coercion.
Pressure to move communication to an out-of-band / personal channel (avoids monitored email).
Imperative request to buy gift cards or send cryptocurrency — a hallmark social-engineering payment lure. Excludes negated third-person advisories ('never ask you to buy...').
Pretext request for the recipient's (second-person) login / MFA code / password. Second-person scoping avoids flagging policy prose ('never share your MFA code').
Vishing / deepfake-voice pretext: caller claims an urgent verbal authorization or an excuse for not video/meeting.
Spoofed-identity / lookalike pretext: new vendor banking details or 'updated' account change request.

verdict_rule: malicious if any pattern matches; classification is advisory (data), routed to action_on_match. False-positive policy: normal business correspondence and third-person descriptions of fraud must NOT match (see verify suite).
